So That I was immersed in And I got really good at it really quick like in 12 months I mean that's probably one of the best opportunity writers in the country within 12 months because I buried myself in it So that's one of the Narrowing your own mind what qualified is So Qualified means what? What does it mean to you? Just yell out don't run up to the mics. What is qualified me? You're an expert really? Yeah, there you go qualified means you're able to perform Doesn't necessarily require you to be an expert might but not necessarily brain surgery. Yeah Well, that's yeah sort of it's the ability to perform whatever that job is so some writing is Isn't even tied to making money. Unfortunately. It's tied to other things right but my world. Yeah, it's tied to making money Okay, so it's the ability to perform and get a desired and satisfactory result. That's it Hey, it's not do you have degrees? It's not do you have certifications? It's not tenure It's not number of years. It's not it's not a it's can we get results? Okay, and so sometimes that standard is pretty low By the way In many cases, it's pretty low a either because they're missing something that isn't that hard to fix or they're at a level where pretty much Anything will work Hey, so like you take what I just showed you with the chirops Okay, so it's instructive Hey, because it blots over in other businesses. So now don't look at me look at everybody else in the room So how many people have been to a chiropractor raise your hands? Now keep them up if you're currently going to a chiropractor Hey, just see all the hands go down Okay, so what does that tell us it tells us chiropractors either aren't doing anything or crappy at what they do to keep Patients over a long period of time We don't have to be very good frankly to improve that result Because in most cases they're not doing anything So any recall campaign will produce a result Hey, even a small case value is 1500 $2000 Right, so we could mail a thousand pieces get one and We would get a satisfactory result So how qualified do you have to be to do that? not very But you're qualified enough to take money for it because you can produce the desired result Hey So that leads us to this get a depth at finding certain wins for the client We're talking a lot about this in strategy, but the task here is really to produce wins We only need to be qualified enough to produce a win So give you simple example I'll often find a client who's barely doing any follow-up So But we call appointment no sale So somebody comes in they get pitched Fire alarm business perfect example So my client a fire alarm business Mostly I do recruiting stuff for them right to get dealers But they're direct sales come So their entire experience of life is rep comes into house Shows you the crying dog film the burn victim pictures scares the hell out of you and sells you $8,000 worth of firearms to hang in your house their entire industry religion is If you don't close that sale then You ain't closing Because it's like a two-hour emotional build and then a bruising clothes And they're like rung out at the end of that and if you don't get them then you ain't get So they do nothing 50 years the industry has never done anything After that pitch Nobody does anything So what if we try to send him a lot? Said you're out of you. So what if we try to send him a lot? Well, what a way so Like three reasons you didn't buy here's the answers to those three reasons He might have forgot to tell you about the hundred and twenty-two monthly payment plan and Oh, by the way, if you respond to this letter within ten days Will also give you this stuff Well, that's not work So a thousand dollar alarm sale if you take the rep out Right you do it direct the profits like 7,640 dollars okay, they're metal canes for God's sakes what fuses on It's an elegantly simple product and it's a metal can with a wound up spring inside And a fuse stuck on it just like is on the sprinkler systems in this room works the same way Certain amount of heat the thing pops off in this room water comes out With these in your house the thing pops off the spring unwinds bangs against the can makes all kind of noise. That's the whole product very clever The only thing costing money is a little fuse So we got like seven dollars dollars profit Perfect for me right because I know we can get really crappy spots and still make money right? I mean that's like terrific, so I Feel like there's a win here, right? so I Get him to do it and I forget the exact result. I think we said I don't know four thousand five thousand and two people bought alarms Well, that's like huge they're like having an orgasm right So now five-step follow-up campaign right So now we do a price drop or if you can't have a full system You should at least have three alarms one here one there one the other place We'll sell you a package of three for this right I got to hold back in business going Okay, they're thrilled Okay, so I found the place to win By the way, what they'd like is Effective direct mail that'll actually get appointments for the reps Nobody's conquered that me included and I kind of thought it was unconscionable, but here's a place to win So you get adept at Finding places where you can win and that's where you want to go Why your trade not so client control? 90% of the time they will come and ask you to do something That is not the certain win 50% of the time they'll ask you to do something that's a certain lose Because they want like their worst most difficult problem solved that nobody else has been able to solve and now they want you to solve it Well, if that's where you agree to play You're putting your head out there to be chopped off bad idea You want to find a place you can you have a high likelihood of winning and it usually won't be the one they show you right out of the gate Not being justified You What does justify a fee you Gotta give some thought to that okay, so most people think You justify a fee by longevity That's the union worker mentality The longer I'm doing this the more I should get paid to do it Which is why we have no industry in America Because it does if you're pushing a broom it doesn't matter if you've been doing it for 30 years or three years But that the task of pushing a broom is only worth about six bucks an hour And if we're going to pay 4850 for it after the guy's been there for 12 years, we're all going to go broke But it's in everybody's head The longer you're doing it the more it's worth Therefore the less time you've been doing it the less justified you are in getting a fee Well, that's nonsense. There's nothing to do with that. Okay, so in every field including this one. I know people I Know copywriters who you know by name we've been doing it for 40 years and I can beat their control drunk. I mean They're like barely competent But they've been around for 40 years And by the way, they feel fully justified and get their fees because they've been around for 40 years Somebody else you could be your first job and you could beat them. So who's justified has nothing to do with time on the job Not what we do Has nothing to do with credentials clients don't care Has nothing to do with hours We're gonna charge by the hour No, no, no, see that's how we pay people out there that shovel manure The driver Gets 10% of the purse money and he spends two minutes in the bike The guy who shovels manure an hour and a half a day shoveling manure The driver gets a lot more money for his two minutes he gets for an hour and a half because it's worth more By the way, not because he's any smarter, I know drivers who couldn't clean a stall right It's not that it's because it's worth More so you need to like liberate your thought about What justifies a fee So it says we said before it's the ability to produce desirable results Which will require you to control the game Relevant experience doesn't matter how much experience you have do you have any experience that's relevant to this particular task? Your own confidence Your willingness to do research because there's almost nothing anybody's gonna ask you to do that somebody hasn't already done and they've done it successfully We've been selling shit for too long in this country So somebody's already done that Those opportunity ads I showed you so mine dates back to 1992 right his ads just like that that date back to 1902 Somebody already figured out how to sell get rich in real estate. I got perfectly good get rich in real estate ads from the 1920s They're fine nothing wrong with them I So I don't I can go get those and help myself I don't have to sit there with a blank chalkboard and figure it out from scratch and most importantly The value of the outcome of the contribution So the fee is really justified by the return on investment to the client that's how the fees justified right I Client a few years ago I mean I gave him a straight answer how many days did you spend on this said to Should how on earth you justify charging me $300,000 for something you did in two days So I should charge you 300,000 for just answering the question because the answers really valuable Because obviously you don't get it so I should double bill you because if you're running around Spending your money based on how much time it takes somebody to do something You're probably making a lot of mistakes in your business guys go on Yeah, I said but to answer your question. He had 1800 stores But we did is increased each store by one transaction a week. That's 1800 Transactions they're each worth a thousand dollars net Wires your problem with $300,000 Yes All right, I see your point. Of course you see the point. Hey, it's about return on investment to the client It's not about anything else. Why would it be about anything else? being exposed as a fraud Kind of harsh Did you want to volunteer I'm gonna don't want to pick a person who said it want to volunteer I Didn't think so. Oh you do good for you Good for you. Okay First of all, it's pretty harsh. So I this guy's a client for five years. He spent a lot of money with me And I showed him his own brochure Right to assignment Most of the time Now if you're taking and there's reasons to do it But it's not what I'm going to teach. So if you're taking like job board gig Elan's gigs those kinds of You pretty much have no choice. I get it but for the most part clients that you originate for yourself That either come to you or you get him This you got to stop doing So you got to get out of the right to assignment Frame of mind all together For a number of reasons I've already talked about they're not competent to define the assignment They shouldn't be in control of the assignment they don't know what the hell they need to win If they actually knew by the way, they probably would have already figured out how to get it So they are not competent to define the assignment And I don't care by the way if we're talking fortune 500 or we're talking the restaurant up the street I've dealt with both By the way the fortune 500 guys are actually kind of dumber I think By the way one third of CEOs fortune 1,000 got there through the sales and marketing track Two-thirds got there through other tracks legal beam counter PhD you know whatever so two-thirds of them are like clues One third of them like get it. So like I get a project for Iacocca. He came from sales You ain't gonna fool him and he gets it right but most CEOs I've dealt with they came from the other place So they don't know they don't get this at all But they're like just Victims walking around and had agencies are taking money from them and showing stick figures and they don't know the difference So you can't let them control the assignment. They're not competent to determine the assignment And you got to get in your head. They are not competent to determine the assignment So if you let them determine the assignment, they're setting themselves in you up for failure not success Secondly which we've already addressed they probably aren't going to give you the win The best opportunity to win Right out of the gate this mostly because they don't know it and You want as part of your assignment if not the whole assignment you want the best opportunity to win So you will see later I'm going to take you through I'm gonna give you a whole file of my correspondence And we're going to go through it. I'm going to highlight some things for you, but you'll take it home You'll see in many places how I make this go away and Cut what I call cut it off at the knees before it even starts But the most important thing you want to do is you want to switch to a diagnostic approach to the way you deal with So the client who comes to you and orders pizza Which is what he's doing when he brings you the assignment he's calling in a pizza Want four large pizzas and one with pepperoni and one with this and one with that and the guy takes the order and makes it I Under ideal circumstances Of course pizza is sort of mostly happening like right now So it's hard to fix this in pizza You can only fix it in pizza business when they're talking about a future order like for a party Then they can say Well, wait a minute. This is for a Super Bowl party. Well, let's have a conversation about that How many people are coming? Are they just friends and family or are they clients how important are they to you? Are you doing any decor? What about desserts, but they can they can get diagnostic and they can change that whole order Very good client pizza business. It's very good So what they thought was going to be a $50 order turns out to be a $500 order Because of diagnostic discussion Do it in pizza business we can do it So you need a diagnostic process. I'm going to show you something but You need this conversation where you are looking for illnesses Slash opportunities so that you can take control of the entire assignment and turn One assignment into multiple assignments or into a project If you don't have a diagnostic process You're you're leaving a lot of money on the table and you're actually not serving the client Well either The best control taking mechanism is diagnosis So in medical If you go to the Cleveland Clinic for your annual for your big executive physical, you know, they charge like ten thousand bucks for The first thing they do is they take away your clothes And they put you in a doofus you down So you can't like decide to leave That's the first thing they do Then they run you through six billion tests Before somebody finally sits down with you and says you need this that the other thing Right. It's hard to argue with this that the other thing after you've been run through all the tests If there were no tests and the guy said you need this that the other thing you would question The magic's in the test Same thing here is this diagnostic process. It's harder to disagree because they saw a diagnosis occur So even when you know You've got to go through the gyrations of a diagnostic process Because it's no good if you turn to him at the cocktail party after five minutes of conversation And say shit what you need is a six-step direct mail campaign and a website No good even if you're right and even if you know No good No, no, you need a diagnostic process and the best diagnostic processes are paid for So very early on the advertising business agency business the norm was and still is right Company calls five agencies. They all put together Presentations and they all come in and they show presentations including Spec work Here's the ad we do for your company. Here's what we would do. Here's this campaign idea Here's that so they do all this free work and They come in they do it all for free. I saw it three times. I said, this is the dumbest thing I've ever seen in my entire life Everybody's working for free, you know to start with there's eight of them and there's only gonna be one Gonna get a gig so the odds suck right and And and it turns out if you actually go study this More than 50% of the companies who do this keep the agency They already had and they just take the best ideas from everybody else and they give them an agency Well, I was just making a sense All right, this is no good So I'm not doing this So what else am I gonna do right? I know I'm not gonna do this so Now it started cheap, but from 1977 on The way you start with me is a paid day of consulting Then we determined What we ought to do and what we ought to do in what order in order to get the results that you want so there's money before Anything happens. There's money for the diagnosis The docs don't do the diagnosis for free You pay for all them blood tests You pay for that catskin you pay for that MRI or somebody does They don't do it all for free on the hopes. They're gonna sell you a hard operation No, you pay every step of the way So ideally you want a diagnostic process and you want to get paid for The other biggie on this list is avoid severely limited clients By finances by capability by size of opportunity so We're not going to do piecework We're going to sell projects Hey, you'll see in my contracts. I'll show you There are yes very compensation formulas for different situations So it's always amusing to me to see somebody like publish their rates and They're the same for everybody So four page in an envelope That you're gonna put a stamp on a reply card is 25 on a box. Well, what about degree of difficulty? I Mean so for one client to get that right could take a month For another client it could take ten minutes. What about degree of difficulty? What about the time you're going to invest? What about the size of the opportunity on the other side the value of that asset to the client? So yes, very compensation for varied situations It makes perfect sense You want to use utilize fee plus Compensation formulas we're going to go through a number of them, but the basic important thing you should know about this Lot of people don't get it It's a lot easier to get people to agree to give you money later Then it is for them to agree to give you money now But you should never not get money now But the easy part of this is getting them to agree to give you money later It's money. They don't have yet And they're only going to give it to you if they get some Now they may feel differently about it later That's a whole management issue, but it's easy to get them to agree to it in the first place We should sell percentage above base the chiropractor at 3,000 clients are percentage about base my explain percentage about base, but it was really easy to sell it much easier the charging of my now It's a management issue, but still You should get compensated for preparation one way or another You should get compensation for hard work product You should try and create multiple ways to make money from the same work When they need vendors to implement and you bring vendors to the table you should get paid for that You should get paid for everything The bottom line of all of this is if you get out of bed Everything you do from getting out of bed to getting back in bed That benefits this client you should get paid for Not get paid for one thing and do six things for free bad model Hook client a up with client B. They are both going to make money together You should get paid You made the hookup So for years in my platinum coaching group model the model was they paid a fee to be in the group and any business They did with each other that each paid a point Well, they're in my room I put them together why shouldn't I get a point? Point can be big So you want to get out of the billable hours thing all together so page 29. Let's talk about compensation structure There are fees you already know all about that there are royalties many of you understand royalties Royalties are typically tied to a result growth sales Adjusted growth sales not a good idea to tie it to net James Garner has been dead now for I don't know six seven years. He's still trying to get his royalties on rock There was no net So that's not good but per unit Per per activity per lead per appointment. It's all different ways you can structure this You will have clients in some industries who tell you legally they're not allowed to pay them nonsense There is no such industry just has to be structured in a certain way I learned about I knew you got royalties as a writer writer because I was already gotten I didn't I didn't know you could get him as a copywriter until I heard Halbert say it And it's not like he taught it to me. He just said it because that was his deal and I had with Know that Revelation on I $15,000 and 5% of the gross my head almost came off my shoulders not because of the 15 because of the 5% of the gross I didn't know that right where I come nobody told me this so royalties percentage above base percentage above base Works like this so company is already doing 100 grand a month So that's the base if you want to be kind inflation is 4% So we'll set it at 104,000 a month Now we're going to install my stuff everything you do over 104,000 a month. I get x percentage The work for chiropractors. It was 30% of above base for three years So you do it with a whole company you can do it with a business unit you can do it with a product category You can do it with a media So only with their web stuff if that's where you're going to play only with their direct mail generated sales That's where you're going to play user fees That those are usually paid like per piece You know you're going to mail this thing so you're going to pay a penny for everyone you mail This is significant if you're If you're doing something for somebody that's going to turn around and essentially resell it license it Roll it out to franchise ease or distributors Etc. Now you want I if you're trying to ask me what I can't but make a note so like If I create a campaign for say for Coverings International and they're now going to give it to all their franchise I Want so much for each franchise owner who's going to use it in addition to the fee to prepare it So user fees Continuation fees so so much to do it and so much after the test is over and they continue to use it again Easier to get them to agree to pay you later than it is to pay now and you'll renewal fee You're going to keep using this stuff. You got to pay to renew continuation fees license fees That's when you bicycle something out to a lot of users. We'll talk about that Buyouts for Royal things you'll see in some of my contracts. That's like one of the answers to the griping about is a buyout clause so Within a certain number of days to allow a test period or in some cases at any point They can decide to make the Royalty go away and just write a lump sum check Equity in lieu of royalties or fees. I don't like it in lieu of fees in some cases. I don't mind it in lieu of royalties equity equivalent We'll get to that later today Parks sometimes it's easy for them to give you stuff That doesn't much matter to them, but might matter to you Like I have a good friend who does a bunch of stuff for United Airlines Well, obviously letting him travel is like nothing to them, but it's significant to him so Reduce the royalty by two points that take unlimited air travel. He's a happy camper. They're a happy camper Barter, I mean, you know, we'll write for food kind of approach I Have a client who furnishes whole house doing work for like lazy boy They're happier furnishing the house that they are right check He was gonna have to furnish the house anyway to marry the wife half his age and bought a big house So the time, you know, the timing was good and you know Self-promotion baked in so often I Have and you will engineer for yourselves opportunities where you're doing work for a client Whose cost can also be your customer? So in my case if I'm writing copy for Somebody that's putting together a big conference of dentists Well dentists can also buy my books that can subscribe to my newsletter So I can bake myself in to their marketing one way or another And that's part of the compensation Is I'm gonna get a whack somehow and Advertising to their customers in a way that doesn't get in the way of the primary job Parasite host opportunity we're gonna talk about that at length later and even jointly owned businesses That what your work actually creates an entire Business and you and the client own it so there's a lot of ways to get paid and you want to be paid You want income after the fact one way or another Because otherwise you never have any sort of stability in this thing you have a job But you have to understand you're creating assets that have long life in many cases that they're going to use for a long period of time So that and I showed you Louisa's ad the desperate housewife ad I wrote in 1996 and I still get a royalty check Not isn't a big royalty check Chilly uses it a few magazines Doesn't matter. Okay average month. There's like 60 to 70 Royalty checks in the mailbox some small some bigger doesn't make any difference. They all add up Okay, if you created an asset and they're using that asset five years from now, you should be getting paid Based on the return on investment from that asset. So big thing big thing Make this shift you're not doing work you're creating assets You got to make the shift in your head first then you can get the client to make the shift So you're not doing work you're creating assets So like my language for example It is with clients is rarely narrowed to copyrighting. We got to find a lead get a lead Convince the lead to make an appointment get the person to come into the office Same stuff we got to do the same thing, but the cosmetic Dennis is going to be worth four times as much as the chiropractor because his transactions are four times bigger, okay? client has to have the right mindset Joe Weldon story I told you He has to have a positive mindset about money So just like you do so if you're having a conversation with a cop So like if the client it goes to lunch and there's any question about who picks up the tab if he's not Telling himself to do it. That's like alarm bell one if he calculates the tip and You know, he's calculating it at precisely 17.6 percent dump this guy Okay, I don't care if you're dating him by the way gals or if it's a prospective client He's no good the wrong mindset about money So what he thinks about money is important some diagnostic questions to determine that are useful to Evidence of investments and advertising and marketing a buyer is a buyer is a buyer So you're out of the room getting food and you miss me talking about you there you go And Discipline yourself against weak moments. We all have it We know this person is no good Terrible client an idiot their chief they're go behind maintenance. They're gonna drive us crazy. We take him anyway You know you you got to fight it The temptation is worse when you need them than when you don't but by the way it doesn't go away even when you don't need I think the temptation is still there. Okay, it's like the I'm a friend who just married a guy who's been divorced six times now The first one her fault. I buy it Okay, second one Probably two people's fault when you're up to like four five and six the common ingredient here is him Right, that's the commonality. So she thinks she's gonna fix this guy. Yeah, he'll fix it He's unfixable. I mean I mean you well you could fix that but I mean he's unfixable generally Gelding would help but it's not gonna fix Totally the problem So discipline yourself against weak moments. Here are all the evil temptations That ruin your income Some are obvious Obligation all they've been with me since the beginning So I have to keep charging them 12 cents No It may be time for everybody to move on If you you can be concessionary if you wish if there are cash a client There's more reasons to do it than not but still be careful of this or you wind up But a whole lot of clients that stay for you with you forever who won't pay which you should be getting paid now There's times for everybody to move on By the way vacuum is filled You want a new wardrobe to home clean the closet? If you got a full closet, you ain't gonna get a new wardrobe because there's no place to put anything So if you got the closet full of clients that you've had forever who you can't charge what you should be charging them because they've been with you Since the beginning go get rid of some of them the best thing you can do for everybody Okay, deal right Mama never made more than $12,000 a year in her life. How dare I make X in a week. Hey, it's in a lot of people's way Apply some logic What where is the relationship here between what mama made? Swabbing toilets in 1936 and what you make now for what you do what? huh Gotta disconnect that a Compassion well, that's all they can pay Great decide whether you're running a charity or you're running a business If you want to do pro bono work by all means knock yourself out. It's usually futile But do it, but please do it for like the dog orphanage Or the starving children in plump lump and be sure that the money's actually getting to the starving children in plump plump And not paying for salary and private jets for somebody who should be paying your fees But if you want to do pro bono work do pro bono work, but don't middle this Don't be not getting paid properly because that's all that poor client can pay Go put him with somebody Who needs to do that work for that money? But don't you be doing that work for that money? We're running a business We're not running so I have this conversation the other day Still it's important so a couple of you were at our supercompany you were with me you were there a couple other people When I wasn't on I was meeting with people privately So I use that So guy wasn't even attending the conference drove over to me And he was a client some years ago He now needs to come back He's been since the beginning of the year by a piecemeal teleconsulting time that thousand bucks a half an hour Now he needs stuff done So we have the discussion of what he needs done and Phase one it's a seventy five thousand dollar fee He then proceeds to tell me about his three kids in college and how much it costs to send the kids to college and They had Katrina issues He had to rebuild this and so he's got a story to tell About why he doesn't have seventy five thousand on I Really need to hear the story It's gonna affect me in the least I'm polite I listen to the story But him telling it to me he might as well said I don't want to spend seventy five thousand He could have told me I don't have seventy five thousand dollars because I'll be notes to you I've become a degenerate game the end result is the same. He doesn't have seventy five thousand dollars I'm not going to do anything for less than seventy five thousand dollars We could have had a much shorter meeting, but I listen to his whole story the end Say sorry, you know the I can't do less and Accomplish what you want, and I'm not going to do it for less so Good luck keep me posted you're welcome to keep buying telephone time if you want and maybe we'll do something in the future Next So that was on the first day By the third day He's back with seventy five thousand now Now I don't know did he sell his kids? I don't know what he did right? I have no idea nor incidentally do I care? however In that first meeting here's what was happening, and you must never forget this in every conversation every conversation Somebody's selling and somebody's buying So you go have a conversation with your kid about cleaning his room Somebody's selling and somebody's buying Okay, if he convinces you that he shouldn't have to clean his room can't clean his room Shouldn't clean his room until Tuesday can't clean his room right now because his buddies are right now outside to go play say he sold you bought Okay, a sale was made Everybody's son Almost every conversation both parties are selling Somebody's better at it than the other one That's all that's going on So he's trying to sell me on why I should do his stuff For less than seventy five thousand dollars I'm trying to sell him on why you should spend seventy five thousand now Now there's three outcomes out of that conversation that are possible one I make a sale To he makes a sale Three neither one of us makes a sale The only outcome that's bad is he makes a sale. That's a very bad outcome Now he's along oh he's got other reasons too. He's a longtime client. He's been with me forever And I remember when yada yada yada and my kids are in college and my shed Was flushed away and so doesn't matter how many reasons he's got four ten fifty who cares It's seventy five thousand now More often than not What happened happens? They miraculously find the money and they come back they don't that's okay. There's another boat coming But the worst outcome is being doing that for 45 thousand All kind of reasons it's bad precedent Abbot Do it once you'll do it again Don't think you won't Future with him Work in the way now of better work that was common In my case working away of working on something on mine I can go I can go right for me I don't need to write for you if you don't want to pay me I can write for me I got plenty of stuff to sell I got lists of stuff. I haven't created yet that I could go right for So bad all the way around So miraculously without even leaving the conference in 48 hours. He managed to find seventy five thousand Wow I Said to him you know what I'll bet you there's another seventy five thousand dollars You could find in your business if you went hunted for it since you found this seventy five thousand so quick and easy Well he laughed I mean he got it okay So most of the time that's all they can pay is not all they can pay that's my point That's all they will pay Maybe but that better be their determination Not yours We predetermined what they'll pay You do that all that size of business that's all they'll pay Although there's a variation of this in every business The all that that's all they'll pay idea Almost every price is elastic Almost every person will pay more than what you think they'll pay and They'll go spend that money on something else if you don't get it by the way So it's not like you don't take it and they put it away for their retirement. That's not what happens. They spend it anyway Go to the trailer park drive around watch Things tilted like this the doors hanging off and if you look through the door there's a 86 inch plasma TV Propped up on two metal TV tables Okay, they're buying what they want Those fan don't think they want it's in your head Resume building. Oh, I'm gonna take these clients because I need to build my resume There's some validity to that Just don't overdo it So like One in a category is sufficient You don't need you don't need to over build the resume It's like exposure thing I Got that when I first started doing the big events Well, you know, you're gonna build this whole list of all these famous speakers you'll be seen with Yeah, but Once with Colin Powell, I don't need ten with Colin Powell. I can say Colin Powell after the first one So we better figure out a way that this makes some money Because otherwise I'm doing it once Great it's on the resume on to the next thing The cash a client validity just don't overdo it So for me for many years got the rancor was a cash a client Having got the rancor as a client in the infomercial world brought me a lot of other clients And they were gracious about it never gave me any grief about competitors testimonials clients could call and they would talk to them So there was non monetary compensation there of significance and Therefore they paid less Than just about anybody else for a long period of time at some point though we had to have the It's been fun But I don't really need the cashier anymore. I need the cash Sorry But you got to at least come up to here you should be here, but you got to at least come up to here And if you take them up to here, it's okay lots other people But for a long time they paid less than anybody They're now paying for both but same as anybody else But there's validity Denial of what the business is gets in your way. I Just want to write it's not what the business is That's what the job is So if you want to write, that's all you're willing to do get a job But this business is not writing The writing is what we get to do by doing the business Of starting stop marketing so all that gets in your way Here is a big biggie biggie biggie biggie Your success has much more to do with the selection of the client Then with you So the clients critically important Three biggest things are their appreciation of value So their mindset about money and whether or not they'll they buy in with you to the concept of We're creating assets here Here's what we're doing here their appreciation of value So like the car the main car I drive now not my collector cars, but my regular car Is a Ford Explorer 2006 I don't put a lot of miles on gift My calls up is be gift all that the lifetime achievement award Made so much money. I'm not paying you enough mechanic are with you How about a new expedition I don't want an expedition too big won't fit in a garage Just give me a spore a head Explorer. I'm happy with it. It's a fourth car. I've been given from clients in 25 years It's not giving you stuff If they are not saying thank you if they're you either have the wrong clients or you're mismanaging a second Size scope and number of opportunities. Now those are three things So you want to think about all three of them? It's a client of sufficient size is their opportunity of sufficient size Is their ambition of sufficient size? Scope means sort of the width of the opportunities are they one media are they ten are they Are they only using local media or are they using national media? Are they global media? So like we have a member he was at the super gun Great member great market. He owns a dairy. They still deliver milk on trucks So they own the whole deal cows the cow the farm the milk trucks It is local area great business great marketer, but no good to me it's a client The Size of the business is too small the size of the market is too small the scope of the business is too small unless and until He decides thanks to me That we're going to sell ice cream all across the country and ship it in freezer chest Now he's interesting to me Just look what just happened The size of the business got bigger the size of the market got huge and The scope just got big Websites catalogs Club program ice cream thing of the month Events so everybody's getting the ice cream once a year gets to come to the farm Bill Cal get the picture taken is that all sorts of shit? So now he was no good as a client for he's great now perfect Ability to execute they got to be able to do it Okay, they have to actually be able to do what you give them to do So don't need to execute You're looking at four things resources Do they have the money? Worst thing in the world Is somebody who scrapes up the money to pay you but then can't buy stamps No good Right, so like in direct mail specific to that if we start talking about a 50,000 piece test And they start talking about do we do the test with 5,000 pieces? This is not good news Even if they have the ability to pay you they have to have the resources to execute And you have to be willing to have a candid conversation about what all that means right You don't want them to be able to run one test and then it either worked or it did Info Martial Business five times before we make one work. So you got to be able to buy immediate buy five times if I lie to you or let you Convince me and you do the show, but you can only test it in Tupelo with two o'clock in the morning once What are my chances of winning not good? I know good so money Staff they have the people they need is it enough of a priority for them so their resources What are the holes in the bucket in their business that will sabotage the whole thing? So for example, that's like the handling of the inbound phone so if what you're if you're writing copy for marketing that drives people to call and The handling of the phone call is for horrid You're getting blamed for the results. You can't have a win So they got a big hole in their bucket. Are they willing to fix it? And then ideally you bring them somebody to fix it and you get paid. That's ideal. We'll get to that Knowledge understanding competence. I mean are they fundamentally competent at what they do Losing proposition to do great advertising for a terrible business You won't sustain the relationship because the business can't sustain itself Hey Evidence of implementation meaning I want to see if they've been able to implement some So I know they can implement this song What are they done? So these are things we got to know because There's a big one to write down I'll say it more than once Good marketing can't overcome bad math So you write the greatest copy in the world, but if that client has fundamentally bad math in their business You lose You need good math The best math is Here's a definition of best math Really bad response is good That's the definition of great math So we can get one one bazillionth of a percentage of response and everybody's happy That's great math it exists in a lot of business as you just have to look for It's great math. I hate the situations where they're worrying about The difference between 2 2.6 percent response 18 percent conversions at the site versus 14.7 percent conversions at the site. No, no, no I want everybody making a ton of dough with like One half of 1 percent conversions. That's what I want So I'm looking for great Size of their prospect pools matter Okay, because otherwise they use them up which means you can't get more work So if they only got 4 000 people in the country they can sell to and you're really good You're gonna burn that out now what? All right, they got 400 000 people they can sell to you can live with them pretty much forever Unexploited segmentation opportunities. They're only doing two things They could be doing six things great. Okay, if they're doing six things already not so great Backend opportunities overlooked. Oh, great. They're doing a good job on the front. They're doing a terrible job on the back May the creative issues. Yeah, the copy sucks. You could make it better So you'll think that way all the time You'll look at a prospective client and you'll immediately be thinking about is the copy good better and different What would I do with the copy? How can I make the copy better? But that's only part of the game That's fine if that exists But by the way, it doesn't have to exist for you to have a great relationship with the client and make a lot of money They can already have great copy and you can have a great relationship with the client and make a lot of money And just being able to give them better copy doesn't necessarily make them a good client Okay, extra pluses and minuses usually not considered Good customers buying from bad marketing Boy, is that a great client?
